vehicle.label: Tiltrotor VTOL Hybrid
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable

parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine
parts.spec: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

parts.label: Nacelle Assembly x 2
parts.spec: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

parts.label: Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2
parts.spec: 25kg-cm DS3218 servo per nacelle, titanium pushrod, dual ball joints, tilt range 0-93deg, crash-tolerant

parts.label: V-Tail x 2 (Ruddervator)
parts.spec: +-35deg V-tail ruddervator pair, MG996R servo each, 30deg dihedral, CFRP sandwich panel, hinge piano wire

parts.label: Landing Gear (Tri)
parts.spec: Tricycle landing gear, spring-steel skid arms, 55mm dia wheels, CG at 1/3 chord, 200mm wheelbase

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide-In)
parts.spec: Belly slide-in battery bay, 6S 8000mAh Li-ion pack, Velcro + strap dual retention, CG position 28% MAC

parts.label: Payload Nose Bay
parts.spec: Interchangeable nose payload bay, Day/IR camera gimbal or survey sensor, 80x60x50mm, 300g payload limit

parts.label: Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay
parts.spec: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access

requirements: Tricycle landing gear with spring-steel skid arms and 55mm diameter wheels
CG at 1/3 chord, 200mm wheelbase
Wing spar carrying nacelle loads into fuselage spine
V-tail/ruddervator and landing gear attach structure
Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings, spar sockets, or mating pads are visible and labeled
Critical slip, snap, pin, hinge, and slide interfaces state plausible clearances
Each subsystem has at least three adjacent interfaces or datums
Fits are named with standard intent: clearance fit for sliding, transition for locating, interference/press only where justified, with a numeric allowance
Holes, slots, and pockets match real catalog hardware sizes (M3/M4 clearance holes, standard bearing OD, common pin diameters)
Tilt axis, bearing seats, servo/linkage access, and nacelle rotation clearance are visible
Wing spar socket, fuselage spine, and payload/battery service paths are separate and inspectable
Output remains educational mockup, avoiding operational flight tuning or control-law details
Tilt mechanism loads route through bearing seats into wing structure via named fittings, and motor/servo modules remain replaceable through access covers

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

physics_paths.path: Nacelle loads -> Wing spar socket -> Fuselage spine -> Landing gear mounts
Avionics cooling airflow -> Avionics bay -> Fuselage spine ventilation channels
Heat from avionics -> Heat sinks -> Fuselage spine -> Radiated to ambient
Wing spar movement -> Wing spar socket -> Fuselage spine
Internal pressure -> Fuselage spine -> External atmosphere
Electrical harnesses -> Harness passthroughs -> Avionics bay
Service access panels -> Avionics bay maintenance

integration_opportunities: Optimize cooling channel layout for better airflow
Enhance structural integrity around spar socket

manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Visual inspection for alignment and fit

physics_paths.path_description: Heat generated by payload electronics is dissipated via convection to ambient air.
Power flows from the power distribution board to the payload electronics via internal harnesses.
Operators can access the payload bay through the service window for maintenance and payload installation/removal.

integration_opportunities.description: Supports various payloads up to 300g within the specified size constraints.
Facilitates easy access for maintenance and payload changes through the service window.
Incorporates thermal fins to enhance heat dissipation from electronic components.

verification_focus.description: Ensure the subsystem does not exceed the 300g payload limit.
Check bolted connections for proper pre-load and torque values.
Verify all electrical connections are correctly made and strain-relieved.
